body.u: I left school at 16 years of age. My first job was a clerical assistant at the local council offices, I didn’t stay there very long as I became quite bored, with the routine. I left them, and went to work for a firm in London. I stayed with my aunt for six months and then rented a flat with a girl who worked in my office.
The flat was quite nice, but it was a bit too expensive to run, seeing that there were only two of us, and our wages weren’t very high.
I was home sick, and missed my former boyfriend very much, so I handed in my notice and came home to live in Sheppey again.

I continued courting with different boys and going out and generally enjoying life until I got engaged at the age of 21. I remember saying to my fiancé before I married I don’t want children for the first two years just in case it doesn’t work. I married at the age of 22 and became pregnant 5 months later!
It was a little girl. At the time I wondered how I was going to cope, but I did and loved every minute of it. By the age of 28 I had 3 lovely children 1 girl and 2 boys.
I continued to work after my children started work and at this job I stayed 5 years.

We were comfortable, with a nice house, and we went out quite often of a night time. This kind of life went on until I reached the age of 41, and I became pregnant again for the fourth time. I was very pleased, as my eldest daughter was twenty years of age. I had another girl. Three years later my first grandchild was born and this is when you begin to feel old, my mother became a great-grandmother and myself a nanny as well.

Unfortunately the following year my father died. This upset me very much and my mother suffered a great deal. I nursed her until she finally came to live with me. It wasn’t a strain as she was no bother.

Now at the age of 68 I look back on my life, was it exciting? Had I fulfilled my expectations? The answer to these questions still remains unsolved, as I have a few more years to go yet and who know what might happen.
